records.ety: [OE. famen; cf. OF. afamer, L. fames. See Famine, and cf. Affamish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Famished (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Famishing.]

defs: To starve, kill, or destroy with hunger.
To exhaust the strength or endurance of, by hunger; to distress with hanger.
quotes: And when all the land of Egypt was famished, the people cried to Pharaoh for bread.
The pains of famished Tantalus he'll feel.

defs: To kill, or to cause to suffer extremity, by deprivation or denial of anything necessary.
quotes: And famish him of breath, if not of bread.

defs: To force or constrain by famine.
quotes: He had famished Paris into a surrender.

defs: To die of hunger; to starve.
To suffer extreme hunger or thirst, so as to be exhausted in strength, or to come near to perish.
quotes: You are all resolved rather to die than to famish?

defs: To suffer extremity from deprivation of anything essential or necessary.
quotes: The Lord will not suffer the soul of the righteous to famish.
